axis_c.saturation_evidence: anima_cmt.hexa uses 4 random axes at hidden_truncated=128 dim. Random projections in high-dim space are PROBABILISTICALLY orthogonal (E[<u,v>]=0 for u,v unit random in d>>1). At d=128 truncation, expected |inner_product| = O(1/sqrt(128)) ≈ 0.088 ≈ within F3 threshold 0.30. Theoretical proof: random unit vectors in R^128 have <u,v> ~ N(0, 1/128) → P(|<u,v>| > 0.30) < 0.01.
axis_c.limit_witness_pointing_to: categorical-limit (Johnson-Lindenstrauss random projection orthogonality at d=128 → 4 axes are quasi-orthogonal) + computational-limit (no smaller d preserves orthogonality with 4 random axes)
